titel: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on education based on scientific principles.
html:
In this document, the government presents its assessment of the observations, conclusions, and recommendations made by the Swedish National Audit Office (Riksrevisionen) in its report "Education Based on Scientific Evidence – Insufficient Efforts to Support Schools with Knowledge" (RiR 2025:31).

The Swedish National Audit Office reviewed the effectiveness of the state's efforts to ensure that education is based on scientific evidence and proven experience. The overall conclusion of the Swedish National Audit Office is that the state's efforts are not effective.

The Swedish National Audit Office's recommendations to the government are that the government should conduct a review of the tasks and responsibilities of the agencies responsible for supporting schools, and that the government should ensure that government assignments to these agencies have timelines that allow for the use of the best available knowledge.

The government agrees with the observations made by the Swedish National Audit Office. Ensuring that the support provided to schools is based on scientific evidence and proven experience is a high priority for the government. The government also believes that it is important that the support provided by the agencies is relevant, clear, and effective, and that the agencies have effective collaboration. In the continued work, the government will consider what additional measures are needed to ensure that the state's work on education based on scientific evidence and proven experience is effective. An important basis for this work, in addition to the Swedish National Audit Office's review, is the Swedish Agency for Public Management's report on the government's assignment to review the tasks and responsibilities of certain agencies responsible for schools, which will be submitted in March 2026. With this document, the government considers the Swedish National Audit Office's report to be finalized.
tldr: The Swedish National Audit Agency has reviewed the education system and found that the government's efforts to ensure that teaching is based on correct knowledge are not working well enough. The government agrees with this criticism and will now review how government agencies should work better to support schools.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that monitors how the state uses money and resources. They have conducted an audit of how the state ensures that schools utilize proven methods and scientific evidence, meaning that schools should use knowledge that has been proven to be correct. The National Audit Office believes that the state's efforts are not efficient enough.

The government agrees that there are problems. They believe it is very important that the agencies that support schools, such as the Swedish National Agency for Education, provide the right and clear assistance. The government wants the agencies to collaborate better and for their mandates to be clear so that they can do a good job.

Now, the government will investigate what needs to be done to improve the situation. They will look at how the tasks and responsibilities of the agencies should be distributed. An important basis for this work will come from the Swedish Agency for Public Management, which is an agency that audits the state's work, and their report is expected in March 2026.
